For each rational number $r$, define $f(r)$ to be the smallest positive integer
$n$ such that $r = m/n$ for some integer $m$, and denote by $P(r)$ the point in
the $(x, y)$ plane with coordinates $P(r) = (r, 1/f(r))$. Find a necessary and
sufficient condition that, given two rational numbers $r_1$ and $r_2$ such that
$0 < r_1 < r_2 < 1$,
$$ P\left( \frac{r_1 f(r_1) + r_2 f(r_2)}{f(r_1) + f(r_2)} \right) $$
will be the point of intersection of the line joining $(r_1, 0)$ and $P(r_2)$
with the line joining $P(r_1)$ and $(r_2, 0)$.
